mood: Description of the raga's mood/rasa and comparison to common feelings and emotions.
description: meaning the golden bodied one; often dubbed the shuddha scale owing to its swarasthanas; described as an austere Ragam
time_of_day: Description of the best time of day to perform the raga. Morning, afternoon, evening, night, etc.
gamaka_usage: Description of how gamakas are used in the raga. Which swaras are used for gamakas and how they are used, where to use strong or weak gamakas, etc.
hindustani_equivalent: Description of the equivalent raga in Hindustani music. If no equivalent, default to unknown.
western_equivalent: Description of the equivalent raga in Western music. If no equivalent, default to unknown.
notable_features: Its arohana-avarohana has all shuddha swaras. In the asampurna melakarta version (Kanakambari), it is an audava-sampurna raga with arohana S R₁ M₁ P D₁ S and avarohana S N₁ D₁ P M₁ G₁ R₁ S. Lavangi is noted as a recent addition to Carnatic music by Dr. M. Balamuralikrishna. Koteeswara Iyer's composition is part of his magnum opus Kanda Ganamutham, in which he composed a kriti in every melakarta raga. Sreesa Putraya is part of a series of compositions created by Dr. M. Balamuralikrishna in every Melakarta ragam. Sri Ganeswaram is from Janaka Raga Varna Manjari.
